subject: Re: Black micrographs for 90% of data set
body:
ahh....that would make sense.....we were just using it to run the 
screen.....or so we thought !!  will give it a go,

   j
  

On Friday, July 27, 2018 at 8:37:51 PM UTC+1, Dimitry Tegunov wrote:
>
> Hi Jon,
>
> I'll give it more thought later, but I think the problem is the 2nd GPU in 
> the system which has significantly less memory than the 1080 Ti. This would 
> also explain why only every Nth movie's processing crashes – the 1080 is 
> doing fine. Can you disable it (uncheck in the window bar) or physically 
> remove it from the system and try again?
>
> Cheers,
> Dimitry
>
> On Friday, July 27, 2018 at 7:43:03 PM UTC+2, Jonathan Grimes wrote:
>>
>>
>>   Hi DImitry
>>
>>    So our machine has with 16 sandy bridge cores with 64Gb, and a 1080Ti graphics 
>> card.  It has a 10Gbit 
>>    card connected to 10Gbit backbone which works well with out 
>> microscopes and core computing.
>>    This is just a test machine, but we wanted to see whther the 128Gb you 
>> suggest was because of the 4 GPUS
>>    on your suggested hardware......from the screen shots attached this 
>> doesnt seem to be a problem.
>>
>>    We also copied over 10 movies from our cluster to the PC.....to see if 
>> the network was an issue.....
>>    and we saw the same behaviour. 
>>
>>    We have now tried it with 2 VPP data sets and one non-VPP data set, 
>> with similar results.
>>
>>    Ive attached our settings, as well as screen shots of some of the 
>> results, including some of the 
>>    hardware monitoring.
>>    
>>    Many thanks 
>>    Jon
>>
>>   I can send you links to some data next week.  
>>     
>>
>> On Thursday, July 26, 2018 at 6:34:04 PM UTC+1, Dimitry Tegunov wrote:
>>>
>>> Hi Jon,
>>>
>>> my first guess would be the GPU running out of memory. Could you please 
>>> describe your hardware configuration and processing settings? I can also 
>>> look at one of the problematic movies if you upload it somewhere.
>>>
>>> Cheers,
>>> Dimitry
>>>
>>>
>>> On Thursday, July 26, 2018 at 5:16:33 PM UTC+2, Jonathan Grimes wrote:
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> We are trying to process 2 data sets collected using VPP and Falcon III 
>>>> detectors. 
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> Previous processing with motioncorr2 gave nice results but the 
>>>> processing in warp gave black micrographs in the real Space tab for ~90% of 
>>>> the data set. 
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> Did you already observed this issue?
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> Is there something that we are missing.  i can post the saved settings 
>>>> if this will help
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> thanks
>>>>
>>>> jon grimes
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
